differentials.name: acute epigastric pain radiating to back / chest (peaking after a few hours, resolving in 2-3 days); nausea, vomiting
differentials.raw: acute epigastric pain radiating to back / chest (peaking after a few hours, resolving in 2-3 days); nausea, vomiting

differentials.name: ⇑ serum amylase + lipase in blood + urine
differentials.raw: ⇑ serum amylase + lipase in blood + urine

differentials.name: hematocrit, C-reactive protein, LDH, serum / urinary trypsinogen, cytokines
differentials.raw: hematocrit, C-reactive protein, LDH, serum / urinary trypsinogen, cytokines

differentials.name: CT severity index + pleural effusion ◊  Serum amylase + lipase useless to judge severity!
differentials.raw: CT severity index + pleural effusion ◊  Serum amylase + lipase useless to judge severity!

differentials.name: Second / late clinical phase beyond 1st week that may persist for weeks - months;
differentials.raw: Second / late clinical phase beyond 1st week that may persist for weeks - months;

context: =  inflammatory disease of pancreas producing temporary changes with potential for restoration of normal anatomy and function following resolution Incidence:  > 300,000 hospitalizations annually in USA Clinical definition of acute pancreatitis: Clinical phases (revised Atlanta Classification System 2012): Pathology:  early inflammation → peripancreatic edema / ischemia → resolution / permanent necrosis / liquefaction Severity:   based on Marshall scoring system (a)  mild = organ failure resolves within 48 hours (b)  severe = organ failure beyond 48 hours Other useful severity markers: Mortalit
